data.title: Canadian Trips to U.S. Drop 32% as BMO Sees No Rebound in Sight
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Canadian travelers continue to shun the United States destinations, said Bank of Montreal (BMO).

Return trips fell 32.4% in the past year to July, with no sign of a turnaround, noted the bank based on data from Statistics Canada.

Return trips by car, usually one-day excursions, plunged 35.8%, while trips by air are off 16.2%.

Meanwhile, the U.S. residents made 3.0% fewer trips across the northern border, a loss in visitors that is mostly offset by increased tourism from other countries (8.3%), pointed out BMO.

The "Buy Canada" movement doesn't seem to be letting up, suggesting consumers will provide timely support to the economy again this quarter as per the previous one, according to the bank.

data.title: Eli Lilly Reportedly Sells Imclone Systems to Celltrion for $330 Million
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- (Updates with Eli Lilly's response in the fourth paragraph.)

Eli Lilly (LLY) has sold Imclone Systems to Celltrion's US unit for $330 million, media outlets reported Tuesday, citing a regulatory filing in South Korea.

Celltrion reportedly said it plans to complete the factory acquisition process by the end of the year.

The two key products that the company sells in the US, as well as future launches, are expected to be shielded from US tariff exposure following upgrades and expansions to the facility, Reuters reported.

"Lilly has an agreement in place with Celltrion to purchase our Lilly Branchburg manufacturing site as part of our broader global manufacturing strategy," a Lilly spokesperson said in a statement e-mailed to MT Newswires. "Following a comprehensive assessment, we concluded that the Branchburg capacity will not meet our future needs."

Celltrion didn't immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

data.title: Sinopec Oilfield Unit Signs $359 Million Construction Contract
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Sinopec Oilfield Service's (SHA:600871, HKG:1033) unit Sinopec International Petroleum Services signed a $359.1 million contract with TotalEnergies Latavi Project Company for a construction project, a Wednesday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The work will include the design, procurement, supply, construction, and commissioning of the phase II Well Site and Interwell Pipeline Project in the Latifiya oil region in Iraq.

Another unit of the firm, Sinopec Oil Engineering and Construction, will be responsible for the implementation of the project.

The scope of the project includes five new production and reinjection well sites, as well as the transformation and upgrading of 11 old well sites and 44 gathering and transportation pipelines.

data.title: Alibaba Shares Surge as It Boosts Bet on AI, Rolls Out New Model--Update
data.description:
By Tracy Qu and Sherry Qin


Alibaba Group shares surged after the company pledged to invest more than $53 billion in artificial intelligence--above its original target--and released a new model.

The Chinese tech titan's Hong Kong-listed stock rallied on Wednesday, helping it more than double gains so far this year. Shares closed 9.2% higher at HK$174.00, its highest level in four years and outperforming the Hang Seng Tech Index's 2.5% advance.

The Hangzhou-based company's latest upswing came after Chief Executive Eddie Wu said the company will increase spending on AI and cloud infrastructure above its February pledge of 380 billion yuan, equivalent to $53 billion, deploying the funds over three years. He didn't provide a new target.

"The AI industry has developed much faster than we expected, and demand for AI infrastructure has also far exceeded our expectations," Wu said.

Fueling further optimism was Qwen3-Max, the latest addition to Alibaba's flagship AI model series, which it describes as its "largest and most capable" large-language model yet.

The preview version ranked among the top three globally on third-party benchmarks LMArena leaderboard, placing it alongside Google's Gemini, Anthropic's Claude, and OpenAI's ChatGPT.

Qwen3-Max, with more than one trillion parameters, boasts strong coding and agentic capabilities, the company said in a social-media post.

Alibaba is also doubling down on building homegrown chips, a crucial foundation for training and deploying the Qwen3-Max model. By advancing such models, alongside its homegrown chip ecosystem, the tech giant is signaling its ambition to control the full AI stack--from hardware to cloud services to application.

Alibaba's push comes as Chinese tech giants are racing to develop LLMs to rival offerings from foreign companies like OpenAI and Anthropic.

Baidu, Tencent Holdings and ByteDance have all launched their own products, while homegrown startups such as DeepSeek are rapidly gaining ground.

Morningstar analyst Chelsey Tam said Alibaba's larger capital-expenditure guidance is a good sign that demand for AI infrastructure is even stronger than expected.

"More demand means a more positive outlook," Tam said.

Nomura's Jialong Shi added markets seem to be viewing the management's outlook for AI demand in China as upbeat. That might have soothed worries about increasingly tough competition in the AI space and the potential formation of an asset bubble as more companies enter the space and investors pile into stocks.

Another tailwind for Alibaba's stock came in the form of news that two of Cathie Wood's Ark Invest funds reopened positions in the tech company, signaling a rekindling of interest from abroad in Chinese tech.

The two funds' investments in Alibaba's American depositary shares total around $16.3 million, according to holdings reports.

Securities and Exchange Commission filings show that this is Ark's first investment or proxy voting record in Alibaba since September 2021, when the Chinese internet giant was grappling with a regulatory crackdown on the country's tech sector.

Alibaba's investment plan and model release add to a broader upswing in Chinese tech stocks, which have been powering equities markets onshore and offshore as advancements bolster investors' conviction in China's AI sector.

In Hong Kong, SMIC--the largest contract chip maker in China--rose as much as 7.8%, while China's No. 2 foundry, Hua Hong, advanced as much as 9.0%. Naura Technology, the country's biggest chip-equipment maker, climbed by the 10% daily limit.

"China is building up a self-contained virtuous cycle" in the AI supply chain, Morningstar analyst Phelix Lee said.

A flurry of AI progress by Chinese tech giants "signals stronger confidence in the performance of China-made models, which supports demand for semiconductors, benefiting both foundries like SMIC & Hua Hong and upstream equipment plays," he said.


--Jason Chau contributed to this article.


Write to Tracy Qu at tracy.qu@wsj.com and Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

09-24-25 0647ET

data.title: OpenAI, Oracle, and SoftBank Announce Plans for Five new AI Data Center Sites
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- OpenAI said Tuesday, it along with Oracle (ORCL) and SoftBank, is planning five new US AI data center sites under Stargate, its AI infrastructure platform.

The company said three sites will be in partnership with Oracle, while the other two will be with SoftBank.

OpenAI added that the combined capacity from these five new sites-along with its flagship site in Abilene, Texas, and ongoing projects with CoreWeave-brings Stargate to nearly 7 gigawatts of planned capacity and over $400 billion in investment over the next three years.

The company said that this puts it on a clear path to securing the full $500 billion, 10-gigawatt commitment it announced in January by the end of 2025, ahead of schedule.

OpenAI said it first announced the $500 billion commitment along with Oracle, and SoftBank in January at the White House alongside President Trump, as part of a broader push to spur investment in American AI infrastructure.

It added that it had entered into an agreement with Oracle during July to develop up to 4.5 gigawatts of additional Stargate capacity, which represents a partnership that exceeds $300 billion between the two companies over the next five years.

It further noted that the new Stargate sites being developed by Oracle will join its flagship Stargate campus in Abilene, Texas, which is already up and running on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.

data.title: Iberdrola CFO we have some space but not for big M&A
data.description:
Sept 24 (Reuters) - Iberdrola's Chief Financial Officer Jose Sainz says:

  • We have some space but not for big M&A

data.title: Nvidia Weighs New Chip Leasing Deal with Microsoft-Backed OpenAI
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Nvidia (NVDA) is discussing a new business model with Microsoft- (MSFT) backed OpenAI to lease its AI chips rather than sell them, The Information reported late Tuesday, citing sources familiar with the talks.

The leasing arrangement is designed to help OpenAI manage high computing costs while avoiding the need to raise large sums for chip purchases, with OpenAI estimating savings of 10% to 15% on server chip costs, the report said.

This also allows Nvidia to offer its components to smaller clients who can not afford the tech giant's chips, the report added.

The leasing model may reduce risk for Nvidia by setting up an entity to buy the servers and use the chips as collateral for loan repayments, the report said.

OpenAI currently leases Nvidia chip servers from Microsoft and Oracle (ORCL), but it is unclear how much a direct lease from Nvidia could cost, the report said.

Nvidia did not immediately respond to an MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Market Talk: Are markets tuning Fed's Powell out?
data.description: STORY: Find out what's moving the markets - and why - with Market Talk, Reuters' daily chat with an investor, economist or market analyst.

data.title: Alibaba Details Global Expansion Plans, Nvidia Integration; Shares Up Pre-Bell
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Alibaba Group's (BABA) cloud unit said Wednesday it intends to launch its first data centers in Brazil, France, and the Netherlands, with more data centers planned for Mexico, Japan, South Korea, Malaysia, and Dubai in the coming year.

The company will also establish new regional service centers in Indonesia and Germany.

Alibaba Cloud's Platform for AI announced an integration of the full suite of the Nvidia (NVDA) Physical AI software stack to help give developers a platform to accelerate humanoid robotics and Physical AI advancements, the company said.

Alibaba shares were up 9.1% in recent premarket activity, while Nvidia rose 0.7%.

data.title: Rupee shielded by central bank intervention even as pressure lingers
data.description:
MUMBAI (Reuters) - The Indian rupee ended nearly flat on Wednesday as likely intervention by the Reserve Bank of India helped stave off pressure on the local unit amid worries over steep U.S. tariffs and a visa fee hike.

The rupee closed at 88.69 against the U.S. dollar, a whiskey away from its all-time low of 88.7975 hit on Tuesday.

Traders said that the central bank likely intervened across segments such as non-deliverable forwards (NDF), currency futures and the OTC spot market to help support the rupee.

The RBI did not immediately respond to an email seeking comment.

The central bank "was present everywhere, leading to muted price-action" despite lingering pressure on the Indian currency amid a broad-based pick up in dollar strength, a trader at a state-run bank said.

While dollar demand was relatively subdued compared to Monday, "the rupee's path of least resistance seems to be towards further weakness," the trader added.

The central bank has stepped up its presence in the offshore NDF market to support the rupee, Reuters reported earlier this month.

"The RBI appears to be allowing gradual weakening of the rupee, intervening selectively to smooth volatility rather than attempting to defend a fixed level," said Abhishek Goenka, chief executive at FX advisory firm IFA Global.

Dollar demand linked to gold imports coinciding with concerns over the impact of the H-1B visa fee hike has added to pressure on the rupee, according to bankers.

India's benchmark equity indexes, the BSE Sensex and Nifty 50 fell about 0.5% each on Wednesday, with the IT index down 0.7%, reflecting investor nervousness about the impact of the change in the H1-B visa fee.

The dollar gained 0.3% against a basket of peers and was last at 97.6 while most Asian currencies dipped. India's benchmark 10-year bond yield, meanwhile, nudged higher to 6.486%.

(Reporting by Jaspreet Kalra; Editing by Sonia Cheema)

By Jaspreet Kalra

data.title: Crude Oil Prices Little Changed on Mixed Demand Signals
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Crude oil prices were little changed on Wednesday amid mixed demand signals, as a decline in U.S. crude oil stocks clashed with a prevailing oversupply outlook.

Brent crude at last look was at US$67.54/barrel and West Texas Intermediate crude was at US$63.29/b. This comes after prices saw gains from the stalled resumption of Kurdish oil exports and Chevron's curbed oil exports from Venezuela due to U.S. permit issues, Reuters said in a Wednesday report.

Exports from Iraq's Kurdistan would have resulted in additional pipeline shipments of about 230,000 barrels per day of oil to Turkey, the report said. Flows have been on pause since March 2023.

Official U.S. government energy data is scheduled to be released on Wednesday and is expected to show an increase in crude oil and gasoline stockpiles in the week to Sept. 19, a Reuters poll of analysts estimated.

data.title: Boyd Gaming Reports Data Breach After Cybersecurity Incident
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Boyd Gaming (BYD) said Tuesday that an unauthorized third party recently accessed its internal IT system, removing information about employees and a limited number of other individuals.

Upon detecting the incident, the company said it responded with the assistance of external experts and in cooperation with federal law enforcement.

The company is currently notifying affected individuals. It added that it does not expect the incident to have a material adverse effect on its financial condition or its business operations.

data.title: Alibaba to Reportedly Increase AI Spending; Shares Up Pre-Bell
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Alibaba Group (BABA) plans to increase its spending on artificial intelligence development as the Chinese e-commerce giant looks to become a "full-stack AI service provider," news outlets reported Wednesday, citing Chief Executive Eddie Wu.

"We are actively proceeding with the [380-billion-Chinese-renminbi ($53.35 billion)] investment in AI infrastructure, and plan to add more investment," Bloomberg reported, citing Wu.

Earlier this year, the company reportedly outlined a plan to spend more than 380 billion renminbi on developing AI models and infrastructure over the next three years.

An Alibaba spokesperson didn't immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

The company's shares were up 9.3% in recent premarket activity.

data.title: SocGen's Overnight Economic News Summary
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Societe Generale in its early Wednesday economic news summary pointed out:

-- US dollar (USD) steady, United States Treasury two-year backs off 3.60% resistance post-auction, stop-through 0.2bp, non-dealer bidding 88.5%. 10s dip to 4.10%.Federal Reserve Chair Powell: near-term risks to inflation are tilted to the upside and risks to employment to the downside, two-sided risks mean that there is no risk-free path; cutting rates too much, too fast could allow inflation to continue to run closer to 3% than to the Fed's 2% target.

-- Australia's consumer price index accelerates to 3.0% year over year in August, above forecast, the highest since July 2024. Core slows to 2.6% year over year. The three-year AGB +8bps to 3.52% (CRT 3.60%), 10-year +4bps to 4.30%. OIS pricing -18bps by the Reserve Bank of Australia before year-end.

-- Day ahead: Germany Ifo business climate. Speakers including Federal Reserve's Daly, Bank of England's Greene. UST five-year auction, Czech central bank (CNB) forecast to stay on hold.

-- Nikkei -0.1%, EUR 10-year IRS steady at 2.70%, Brent crude +0.2% at $67.80/barrel, Gold -0.3% at $3766/oz.

data.title: DP World Takes Delivery of World's Largest, Eco-friendly Carrier
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Logistics provider DP World (DFM:DPW) took delivery of the MV Höegh Sunrise Aurora-class vessel at Jebel Ali in Dubai, United Arab Emirates.

MV Höegh Sunrise is considered to be the world's largest and most environmentally friendly carrier and the first in the automotive shipping sector to feature a zero-carbon ready design, the Dubai Media Office said Wednesday.

The vessel is capable of running on future alternative fuels like ammonia and methanol, and can cut carbon emissions per car transported by 58%, compared with the current industry standard.

data.title: Eni Storage Systems Commences Development of Lithium Battery Facility in Italy
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Eni (ENI.MI) said its Eni Storage Systems joint venture commenced the development of an industrial project for the production of stationary lithium batteries in the Brindisi industrial area in Italy.

The joint venture, held with Seri Industrial, aims to establish a hub with a total production capacity of more than 8 gigawatt-hours per year of lithium-iron-phosphate batteries, according to a Wednesday release.

Italian energy company Eni said the project is currently in the engineering phase, which is expected to be completed in the first quarter of 2026.

data.title: Ford Motor Recalling 115,539 Vehicles Over Potential Steering Control Issue
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Ford Motor (F) is recalling 115,539 vehicles in the US over potential issues with steering control, increasing the risk of a crash, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said Tuesday.

The company said the affected vehicles' "steering column upper shaft may detach from the steering column" and lead to loss of steering control. The recall covers certain 2020 to 2021 models of Ford's F-250, F-350, and F-450 vehicles, according to the statement.

Ford said dealers will inspect and repair or replace the recalled vehicles' upper shaft as necessary, free of charge.

data.title: TSX futures rise after index's halted rally
data.description:
(Reuters) -Futures linked to Canada's main stock index edged higher on Wednesday, after the index fell in the previous session despite posting an intraday record high.

Futures for the S&P/TSX index rose 0.2% to 1,765.40 points by 06:19 a.m. ET (1019 GMT), a day after the index crossed the 30,000 threshold for the first time.

Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said he had "constructive" trade talks with Chinese Premier Li Qiang with both countries seeking to discuss trade conflicts.

Canada has imposed tariffs on imports of China-made electric vehicles and steel and aluminum since last year, with Beijing retaliating with levies on Canadian canola imports.

Separately, Carney said trade negotiations with the U.S. were ongoing, with remaining issues to move to a forthcoming review of the United States-Mexico-Canada trade agreement.

Meanwhile, Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem expressed concern on Tuesday about U.S. President Donald Trump's pressure tactics on the U.S. Federal Reserve.

In company news, Manulife Financial Corp's wealth and asset management arm said it had signed an agreement to buy UK asset manager Schroders' business in Indonesia.

The Trump administration is seeking an equity stake of as much as 10% in Lithium Americas, two people told Reuters. American-listed shares of the company soared as much as 60% in premarket trading.

Bank of Montreal launched a process to sell some of its U.S. branches with about $6 billion in deposits, the Wall Street Journal reported on Tuesday.

In commodities, gold continued to trade near record highs, while copper and iron ore were little changed. Oil prices inched up on concerns of tightening supplies.

FOR CANADIAN MARKETS NEWS, CLICK ON CODES:

TSX market report [.TO]

Canadian dollar and bonds report [CAD/] [CA/]

Reuters global stocks poll for Canada

Canadian markets directory

(Reporting by Twesha Dikshit; Editing by Sahal Muhammed)

data.title: Amazon, SAP Enter New Europe Sovereign Cloud Collaboration
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Amazon (AMZN) Web Services and SAP (SAP) said Wednesday they plan to make SAP Sovereign Cloud capabilities available on the AWS European Sovereign Cloud.

The new independent cloud for Europe is backed by a planned 7.8 billion-euro ($9.18 billion) investment from e-commerce giant Amazon.

The AWS European Sovereign Cloud is scheduled to launch its first region in Brandenburg, Germany, by the end of this year. It will have no "critical dependencies" on non-European Union infrastructure, the companies said.

data.title: Cool Company Reaches Agreement in Principle to Be Acquired by EPS Ventures
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Cool Company (CLCO) has reached an agreement in principle with EPS Ventures for a deal under which EPS would acquire all outstanding shares of CoolCo it does not already hold for $9.65 per share in cash, the companies said Wednesday.

The proposed transaction would be implemented through a cash merger of a subsidiary of EPS with and into CoolCo.

Following completion of the proposed merger, CoolCo would be wholly owned by EPS and seek to be delisted from the New York Stock Exchange.

CoolCo said an independent committee formed to review and negotiate the terms of the potential transaction intends to recommend that the board approve the deal, subject to completion of definitive agreements.

The companies are targeting a Q4 or Q1 closing of the potential transaction. EPS already owns 59.3% of outstanding CoolCo common shares, they said.

Shares of Cool Company gained nearly 20% in premarket activity.

data.title: Applied Materials to Collaborate With GlobalFoundries on Singapore Waveguide Fabrication Facility
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Applied Materials (AMAT) said late Tuesday it will work with GlobalFoundries (GFS) to set up a waveguide fabrication facility at GlobalFoundries' Singapore project to support the growing use of photonics in artificial intelligence technologies.

Applied Materials will develop waveguide components, with GlobalFoundries as its high-volume manufacturing partner in that market, the company said.

data.title: Apollo Global Management Gets Approval for 3 New Evergreen ELTIF Funds in Europe
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Apollo Global Management (APO) said Wednesday that it has received regulatory approval from Luxembourg's Commission de Surveillance du Secteur Financier to launch three new evergreen, semi-liquid European Long-Term Investment Funds.

The company said the new funds include Apollo European Private Credit ELTIF, which will focus on first-lien, senior secured direct lending to large and upper middle-market European companies; Apollo Global Diversified Credit ELTIF, offering a global multi-asset credit strategy across private credit sectors; and Apollo Global Private Markets ELTIF, which will invest in private companies globally through secondaries and co-investments.

The funds will be launched in the coming months via the Apollo Private Markets Umbrella SICAV under the updated ELTIF 2.0 framework, designed to broaden individual investor access to its private markets expertise through tailored, evergreen formats and distribution channels, the company said.

data.title: US Stocks Brief: S&P Futures Up 0.15% and Nasdaq 100 Futures Up 0.3% As Fed Chair Powell Calls Stocks "Fairly Highly Valued"
data.description: (MT Newswires) --

data.title: Suzhou Guoxian Innovation Technology Co., Ltd. announced that it expects to receive CNY 1.99 billion in funding from Visionox Technology Inc. and another investor
data.description:
Suzhou Guoxian Innovation Technology Co., Ltd. announced that it will receive CNY 1,99,00,00,000 in an equity round of funding on September 24, 2025. The transaction include the participation from new investor Kunshan Weixin United Investment Co., Ltd. hold 49.90% stake and returning investor Visionox Technology Inc. The company's registered capital is increase from CNY 10,000,000 to CNY 2,000,000,000. The transaction has been approved at the 24th Meeting of the Company?s 7th Directorate, and does not need the Shareholders?

Approval.

data.title: Copper little changed in quiet trade as dollar strengthens
data.description:
Sept 24 (Reuters) - Copper prices struggled to gain ground despite concerns over tight supply on Wednesday as a firmer dollar weighed on prices amid quiet trade ahead of a week-long holiday in top metals consumer China at the start of October.

Benchmark three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange was down 0.2% at $9,957 a metric ton as of 1019 GMT.

"From a technical perspective, it seems fairly clear it's hit a significant ceiling for the short term," said Dan Smith, managing director of Commodity Market Analytics.

One trader said there had been some fresh long positions in copper, but not enough to move the market. "The price is just stuck at the current level."

The U.S. dollar index strengthened by 0.4%, weighing on the market, even amid lingering supply concerns due to an outage at the Grasberg copper mine in Indonesia.

A stronger U.S. currency makes dollar-priced commodities more expensive for holders of other currencies.

Aluminium slipped 0.4% to $2,627 per ton.

But in the United States, the premium paid to buy aluminium on the physical market - on top of the LME price - was at a record high of $0.74 per lb, or $1,631 a ton.

The premium has almost doubled since the end of May after U.S. President Donald Trump raised the tariff on imports of the metal to 50% from 25%.

CRU senior analyst Alex Christopher said on a webinar this week that the rise in the premium had been slowed by a roughly 150,000 tons inventory build ahead of the increase in tariffs at the start of June.

"We now believe that has been consumed and that's why we're now seeing the full impact of the 50% tariff," Christopher said.

Among other metals, zinc was down 0.1% at $2,884 a ton, even as LME zinc stocks fell to 44,400 tons, the lowest since April 2023. Nickel fell 0.3%, while lead was flat and tin gained 0.3%. (Reporting by Tom Daly. Additional reporting by Dylan Duan and Lewis Jackson. Editing by Janane Venkatraman and Mark Potter)

data.title: Guangdong Construction Engineering Wins Bid for 1.9 Billion Yuan Project
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Guangdong Construction Engineering (SHE:002060) won a bid for the construction of properties in Chepi Village in Guangzhou, China's East Financial City District for 1.92 billion yuan, according to a Tuesday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Shares closed 3% higher on Wednesday.

The construction area covers 284,350.1 square meters, and will comprise foundation pit support, earthworks, foundation and main structure engineering, secondary structure and masonry engineering, steel fabrication and installation, decoration, and landscaping, the filing said.

data.title: Oil gains, lower US crude stockpiles provide floor
data.description:
LONDON (Reuters) -Oil prices rose on Wednesday as an industry report showed U.S. crude inventories declined last week, adding to a sense in the market of tightening supplies amid export issues in Kurdistan and Venezuela and disruptions to Russian supplies.

Brent futures rose 40 cents or 0.6% to $68.03 a barrel by 1000 GMT. U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude futures gained 38 cents or 0.6% to $63.79.

"The market is expecting supply excess and stock builds globally in the last quarter of the year, but the focus recently has shifted back to Eastern Europe and the possible introduction of fresh sanctions on Russia," said PVM Oil Associates analyst Tamas Varga. 

The stalled resumption of Kurdish oil exports along with Chevron's curbed oil exports from Venezuela due to U.S. permit issues added to short-term bullishness in the market, he added.

Both benchmarks climbed by more than $1 a barrel on Tuesday as a deal to resume exports from Iraq's Kurdistan stalled, halting pipeline shipments of what would have been 230,000 barrels per day of oil from the region to Turkey, as two key producers asked for debt repayment guarantees. Pipeline flows have been stopped since March 2023.

Meanwhile, President Donald Trump on Tuesday said he believed Ukraine could retake all the territory captured by Russia, marking a sudden rhetorical shift in Ukraine's favour. The Trump administration had urged EU countries to phase out Russian oil and gas quicker earlier in the month. 

Russia is seeing shortages of certain fuel grades as Ukrainian drone attacks reduce refinery runs, according to traders and retailers, after Ukraine stepped up drone attacks on energy infrastructure to reduce Moscow's export revenues.

Iran's oil minister Mohsen Paknejad said "new burdensome restrictions" on Iran's oil sales wouldn't be added and sales to China would continue, as Tehran and European powers struggle to reach a deal to prevent the return of U.N. sanctions this week.

American Petroleum Institute figures showed U.S. crude and gasoline stocks fell, while distillate stocks rose last week, according to market sources, citing API data.

Official U.S. government energy data is due on Wednesday. A Reuters poll of eight analysts ahead of the inventory data, however, estimates that crude oil and gasoline stockpiles rose in the week to September 19 while distillate inventories likely fell. [EIA/S]

Overall, the global oil market is bracing for elevated supply and slowing demand - in its latest monthly report, the International Energy Agency said world oil supply would rise more rapidly this year and a surplus could expand in 2026.

(Reporting by Seher Dareen in London, Jeslyn Lerh in Singapore; Editing by Bernadette Baum and Ros Russell)

By Seher Dareen

data.title: London stocks mirror Wall Street losses, investors assess corporate results
data.description:
(Reuters) -London stocks were subdued on Wednesday, as investors assessed an overnight sell-off on Wall Street after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's comments and weighed corporate updates at home.

The benchmark FTSE 100 fell 0.08% at 0949 GMT, while the domestically focused FTSE 250 was down 0.07%.

Wall Street's main indexes fell overnight after Powell said the central bank needed to continue balancing the competing risks of high inflation and a weakening job market in coming interest rate decisions, which gave little indication about the future path of interest rates.

Still, investors expect the U.S. central bank to cut interest rates in its remaining two meetings of the year.

In corporate news, Eli Lilly Chief Executive Dave Ricks in an interview with the Financial Times said Britain was "probably the worst country in Europe" for drug prices.

The remarks are part of a broader backlash from pharmaceutical giants, including Merck and AstraZeneca, which have scaled back investments in Britain over the challenging environment.

UK's pharma and biotech sector fell 0.9% in early trading, with heavyweight AstraZeneca declining 1%.

The personal goods sub-sector was the biggest loser, falling 2.7%, with shares of luxury firm Burberry down 3.2%.

Among individual stocks, Britain's JD Sports Fashion reported a 13.5% fall in first-half profit. While sticking to its full-year guidance, the company also flagged ongoing caution over the trading backdrop. Shares of the British sportswear retailer was nearly flat in early trading.

Baltic Classifieds Group's shares dropped the most in the FTSE 250, down 10.6%, after the company reported results below expectations.

Pinewood Technologies slipped 9.8% after the British auto retailer projected its 2025 EBITDA below analysts' consensus, according to Berenberg.

On the flip side, aerospace and defence rose 1% tracking gains in European peers after U.S. President Donald Trump, in a sudden and surprising rhetorical shift, said he believed Ukraine could retake all its land occupied by Russia.

Babcock International Group was the top FTSE 100 gainer, up 1.5%, while BAE Systems rose 1.4%.

(Reporting by Sanchayaita Roy in Bengaluru; Editing by Maju Samuel)

data.title: Trump's H-1B visa fee increase raises US doctor shortage concerns
data.description:
(Reuters) -The Trump administration's plan to dramatically raise fees for H-1B visas is drawing concern from U.S. healthcare groups who say the move could worsen staffing shortages as more than half of healthcare workers consider changing jobs within the next year.

The U.S. Department of Homeland Security is reviewing policy changes that would increase the cost of applying for H-1B visas to as much as $100,000 from the current top of $4,500. The H-1B program allows U.S. employers to hire foreign workers in specialty fields like technology, engineering, medicine, and academia.

The visas are widely used by the U.S. healthcare sector to recruit international medical graduates or foreign-trained doctors and other professionals trained abroad.

The American Academy of Family Physicians emphasized that international medical graduates account for more than one-fifth of practicing family doctors and are disproportionately likely to serve in rural areas.

FEE INCREASE IMPACT ON DOCTOR NUMBERS

U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services reported that in fiscal year 2025 there were about 442,000 unique H-1B visa beneficiaries across all sectors, with 5,640 petitions approved in the healthcare and social assistance industry alone.

The influential American Medical Association warned that fees as high as $100,000 could choke off the international physician pipeline.

"With the U.S. already facing a shortage of doctors, making it harder for international medical graduates to train and practice here means patients will wait longer and drive farther to get care," said AMA President Bobby Mukkamala.

Hospital and doctor groups warned that the fee increase could sharply reduce the number of foreign-trained doctors entering the U.S. system. For many hospitals already stretched thin, that could also mean fewer specialists and higher burdens on domestic medical staff. 

The American Hospital Association said hospitals rely on the program as a short-term measure to fill gaps in the workforce.

"The H-1B visa program plays a critical role in allowing the hospital field to recruit highly skilled physicians and other healthcare professionals to ensure access to care for communities and patients," an AHA spokesperson said, adding that the group is pushing for exemptions to the increase for healthcare personnel.

"Nearly 21 million Americans live in areas of the U.S. where foreign-trained physicians account for at least half of all physicians," the AAFP said.

Many hospital systems have been contending with staffing pressures since the COVID-19 pandemic. Several, including OhioHealth, Cleveland Clinic, Cedars-Sinai, and Mass General Brigham told Reuters they are assessing what the administration's changes will mean for their operations.

The U.S. could be short 13,500 to 86,000 physicians by 2036 as demand grows faster than supply, according to the Association of American Medical Colleges.

(Reporting by Mrinalika Roy in BengaluruEditing by Bill Berkrot)

By Mrinalika Roy

data.title: Keppel Places Dormant Subsidiary Under Member's Voluntary Liquidation
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Keppel (SGX:BN4) placed its dormant subsidiary, Harvestland Development, under member's voluntary liquidation, according to an after-market filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

The liquidation is not expected to impact the company's financials for the year.

data.title: South Korea's MFG buys about 120,000 tons soymeal, traders say
data.description:
Hamburg, Sept 24 (Reuters) - South Koreas Major Feedmill Group (MFG) purchased an estimated 120,000 metric tons of soymeal in an international tender on Wednesday, European traders said.

Two 60,000 ton consignments were bought to be sourced from South America, the United States or China.

One was purchased from trading house Olam at an estimated $335.98 a ton cost and freight (c&f), including a surcharge for additional port unloading.

A second consignment was bought from trading house Cofco at an estimated $336.93 a ton c&f, also including a surcharge for additional port unloading.

The tender had sought arrival in South Korea in February and March 2026. Reports reflect assessments from traders and further estimates of prices and volumes are still possible later.

(Reporting by Michael Hogan)

data.title: HSBC to Redeem Two Series of Notes in November
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- HSBC Holdings (HSBA.L) will redeem its 2.256% resettable notes worth 1 billion pounds sterling due in November 2026, and its 0.309% fixed-to-floating rate notes worth 1 billion euros due 2026, on Nov. 13, 2025.

The British bank said Wednesday that it will redeem the outstanding London-listed notes per calculation amount plus accrued and paid interest, with delisting to follow on Nov. 14.

data.title: A delicate balancing act
data.description:
LONDON (Reuters) -What matters in U.S. and global markets today

By Samuel Indyk, Equities Reporter, Finance and Markets Breaking News, EMEA

Wall Street snapped a three-day streak of record closes as Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell struck a measured tone on future rate cuts, balancing inflation risks with a weakening labor market. His remarks, which offered little clarity on the timing of further easing, triggered a broad pullback in equities, particularly in tech. Asian markets rebounded overnight, led by Chinese AI enthusiasm, while U.S. futures edged higher, suggesting a tentative rebound.

* Powell reiterated the Fed's delicate balancing act between inflation and employment, calling the policy outlook "a challenging situation". While he left the door open for more rate cuts, he gave no timeline, and notably described equities as "fairly highly valued". Traders are still pricing in about a 95% chance of a rate cut at the October meeting.

* The Nasdaq fell nearly 1% as Nvidia dropped 2.8%, reversing gains from its OpenAI investment announcement. Amazon, Microsoft, and Apple also declined. The S&P 500 lost 0.6%, its biggest one-day drop in three weeks, while the Dow slipped 0.2%. 

* Meanwhile, Boeing shares rose 2% after securing an $8 billion order from Uzbekistan Airways, helping cushion the Dow's decline. Micron rose after hours on upbeat earnings and guidance.

* Asian stocks rebounded on Wednesday, with Hong Kong's STAR 50 Index up 3.6%, led by a 9% rise in Alibaba after it unveiled its largest AI model. European shares are a touch softer.

Today's Market Minute

* In a rhetorical shift, U.S. President Donald Trump said he believed Ukraine could retake all its land occupied by Russia on Tuesday, in a social media post following a combative, wide-ranging speech to the U.N. General Assembly that leveled scathing criticism of world leaders.

* Trump on Tuesday also scrapped a meeting with top congressional Democratic leaders to discuss government funding, raising the risk of a partial government shutdown beginning next week. 

* The consensus among economists is that Trump's tariff burden has so far been shouldered by U.S. companies and that consumers have got off relatively lightly, although, writes ROI markets columnist Jamie McGeever, these scales are widely expected to tip the other way in the coming months.

* OpenAI, Oracle and SoftBank  on Tuesday announced plans for five new artificial intelligence data centers in the United States to build out their ambitious Stargate project.

* Jimmy Kimmel returned to U.S. late-night television on Tuesday defending political satire against Trump administration "bullying", six days after his on-air remarks about the murder of right-wing activist Charlie Kirk led Walt Disney to suspend his show.

* The looming revival of international sanctions on Iran is unlikely to curtail Tehran's vital oil exports but could benefit Chinese refiners, offering them access to a larger share of discounted Iranian crude. Read the latest from ROI energy columnist Ron Bousso.

Chart of the day

While much has been made of the performance of the so-called "Magnificent 7" since 2022, European defense stocks have had an even more impressive rally, led by an almost 2,000% rise in Europe's biggest ammunition maker Rheinmetall. Europe's index of aerospace and defense companies has risen threefold since Russia's invasion of Ukraine. 

Suppliers of jets, tanks and shells tend to do well when there are ongoing conflicts. And while Trump on Tuesday asserted that he had already ended seven wars since becoming president for the second time, the war in Ukraine remains a trickier puzzle to solve. 

Today's events to watch

* U.S. New Home Sales (10:00 AM EDT) * U.S. Treasury to sell 5-year notes and 2-year floatingrate notes (1:00 PM EDT) * Fed Governor Michael Barr speech on bank stress testing(1:00 PM EDT) * Senior Credit Officer Opinion Survey release (2:00 PM EDT) * H.4.1 Reserve Balances report (4:30 PM EDT)

Want to receive the Morning Bid in your inbox every weekday morning? Sign up for the newsletter here. You can find ROI on the Reuters website, and you can follow us on LinkedIn and X. 

Opinions expressed are those of the author. They do not reflect the views of Reuters News, which, under the Trust Principles, is committed to integrity, independence, and freedom from bias.

(By Samuel Indyk; Editing by Alex Richardson)

By Samuel Indyk

data.title: Torrent Power Acquires 49% in Newzone India, 100% in Holding Firm for INR2.11 Billion
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Torrent Power (NSE:TORNTPOWER, BOM:532779) said it has acquired nearly 1.2 million equity shares of Newzone India and 3 million equity shares of its holding company, Newzone Power Projects, from the Sarawagi family and related HUFs (Hindu Undivided Families) for a total consideration of 2.11 billion rupees.

The transaction gives Torrent Power 49% equity ownership in Newzone India, while Newzone Power Projects, already holding 51%, now becomes a wholly owned subsidiary, the company said in a Tuesday filing with the Indian bourses.

The acquisition is aimed at implementing a thermal power project on land owned by Newzone India in Annupur district, Madhya Pradesh, India.

The deal is expected to be completed within four months.

Shares of the company fell 1% in recent trade.

data.title: TotalEnergies, RWE Bag Tender for 1.5-GW Offshore Wind Project in France
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- TotalEnergies (TTE.PA) and RWE (RWE.F) won the government tender to develop and operate the 1.5-gigawatt Centre Manche 2 offshore wind farm off the coast of Normandy, France.

The French energy giant expects to invest 4.5 billion euros to build the facility, which is set to be the country's largest renewable energy project, according to a Wednesday release.

The German utility, on the other hand, aims to exit the consortium, so TotalEnergies will pursue the project as its operator and propose to find a new partner. It plans to reach a final investment decision by early 2029 and expects the wind farm to begin generating electricity in 2033.

data.title: Fujian Wanchen Biotechnology Submits Application for Hong Kong Share Listing
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Fujian Wanchen Biotechnology (SHE:300972) submitted its application for its listing on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, according to a Wednesday disclosure to the Shenzhen bourse.

The food company plans to use proceeds from its share listing in the city for its store network expansion, introduction of new products, digital, marketing, and warehousing upgrades, as well as mergers and acquisitions, and working capital, according to documents submitted Tuesday to the Hong Kong bourse.

data.title: Korea Consumer Confidence Strong in September, But Slips Modestly
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- South Korea's Composite Consumer Sentiment Index (CCSI) for September logged at 110.1, down from 111.4 in August but still above the 100 mark that separates optimism from pessimism, reported the Bank of Korea on Wednesday.

South Koreans showed concern in September about prospective domestic economic conditions, with the index reading for that category dropping to 97 in September from 100 in August, and down from 106 in July, according to Bank of Korea figures.

Consumers were also downbeat about current economic conditions, with an index reading of 91 in the category in September, down from 93 in August.

South Koreans were not optimistic about the nation's job-market in September, with the category of prospective employment opportunities index striking 91 in the month, down from 92 in August.

Despite reservations, South Korean sentiments about prospective wages were optimistic, with a reading of 122 in September, down minutely from 123 in August.

South Koreans expect consumer price inflation to post a 2.5% on year for the next year, three years and also five years, according to the Bank of Korea survey.

In August, the Bank of Korea estimated the nation's gross domestic product (GDP) will expand by 0.9% on year in 2025, while inflation will log near 2%.

data.title: Mercedes mulls investment in Geely-backed smart driving firm, Bloomberg reports
data.description:
BEIJING (Reuters) -Mercedes-Benz Group is preparing to invest in Geely-backed autonomous driving system developer Chongqing Qianli Technology, Bloomberg reported on Wednesday.

The German automaker is planning to buy a minority stake in Qianli to bolster its software capabilities in China, with a deal to be announced as soon as this week, the report said.

(Reporting by Beijing newsroom;Editing by Elaine Hardcastle)

data.title: Piccadily Agro Industries says Radico Khaitan restrained by court from using 'Kashmyr' mark
data.description:
Sept 24 (Reuters) - Piccadily Agro Industries Ltd:

  • Files suit against Radico Khaitan for trademark infringement
  • Radico Khaitan restrained by court from using 'Kashmyr' mark

data.title: VEGOILS-Palm ends higher on strong exports, though soyoil spread weighs
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR, Sept 24 (Reuters) - Malaysian palm oil futures inched higher on Wednesday, supported by stronger exports and firmer soyoil, but palm oil losing its competitive advantage over soyoil capped the gains.

The benchmark palm oil contract for December delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange was up 37 ringgit, or 0.85%, at 4,380 ringgit ($1,041.62) a metric ton at the close. The contract fell 2.25% on Tuesday.

Crude palm oil futures traded marginally higher on strong export performance and higher Chicago soybean oil prices at midday, said David Ng, a proprietary trader at Kuala Lumpur-based trading firm Iceberg X Sdn Bhd.

"The narrowing spread between palm oil and soybean oil is also weighing on palm oil price gains."

Cargo surveyors estimated exports of Malaysian palm oil products for September 1-20 to have risen between 8.3% and 8.7% month-on-month.

Dalian's most-active soyoil contract fell 0.44%, while its palm oil contract added 0.44%. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade slipped 0.18%.

Palm oil tracks the price movements of rival edible oils as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

Oil prices inched up as an industry report showed U.S. crude inventories declined last week, adding to a sense in the market of tightening supplies.

Stronger crude oil futures make palm a more attractive option for biodiesel feedstock.

Malaysian palm oil futures are likely to jump 27% to a more than three-year high of 5,500 ringgit per ton in January-March 2026, as rising biodiesel consumption in top producer Indonesia tightens supplies, industry analyst Dorab Mistry said.

The European Union will delay launching its anti-deforestation law for a second time, postponing for another year the ban on imports of commodities such as palm oil that are linked to forest destruction.

($1 = 4.2050 ringgit) (Reporting by Ashley Tang; Editing by Sumana Nandy and Subhranshu Sahu)
